# UC-19.1.21 · Cisco UCS HCL Compliance via Intersight (cond.HclStatus)

> **Criticality:** Medium &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Platform &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Configuration &middot; **Wave:** Crawl &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We check which operating system, driver, and firmware mix each physical server is allowed to run, using the same management service you already trust. When the mix is not on the approved list, we flag it from most to least risky so the right team fixes it before odd storage or network behaviour shows up in production.*

---

## Description

Monitors the Intersight `cond.HclStatus` object (API collection `/api/v1/cond/HclStatuses`, one document per HCL evaluation tied to a server through `ServerMoid`) as ingested on sourcetype `cisco:intersight:condhclstatus` from Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight (Splunkbase 7828). The SPL `coalesce`s JSON paths such as `Status`, `Reason`, `OsVendor`, `OsVersion`, `Model`, `ProcessorPrefix`, `ServerFirmwareVersion`, `ServerMoid`, and `Tags`, drops `Validated` rows, `stats` latest by `ServerMoid` to keep the most recent HCL result per target, tiers `severity_bucket` from Cisco codes (`NotValidated` vs `NotListed` in production, `Incomplete` telemetry gap, `ServiceUnavailable` to the HCL back end), enriches with `intersight_server_inventory` on `server_moid`, and left-joins `cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary` so operators see a display name, inventory model, and serial instead of a bare Moid. Pair with firmware drift in UC-19.1.20 when you need both matrix posture and per-component version equality.

## Value

You gain predictable, Cisco-backed evidence that the full operating-system, driver, and firmware stack on each claimed UCS or X-Series node matches a tested matrix entry—protecting time-to-resolution under support contracts, reducing surprise break-fix around NVMe, FCoE, RDMA, or NIC offload paths, and giving audit teams an exportable posture table that answers “is this build combination supposed to be here?” before change boards approve major OS or driver moves.

## Implementation

Install Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight, enable polling for `cond.HclStatus` and `compute.PhysicalSummary` into `cisco_intersight` on a 15–30 minute cadence, deploy `intersight_server_inventory` with `server_moid` matching Intersight Moids, paste the `spl` into a report and a thresholded alert on `severity_bucket` of critical and high, and review quarterly with the UCS HCL public tool when planning OS minor upgrades.

## Evidence

Saved search and dashboard: `index=cisco_intersight` using sourcetype `cisco:intersight:condhclstatus` with a non-`Validated` posture table, `intersight_server_inventory` lookup join, and the companion join to `cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary` for `display_name` and serial, retained per your index and evidence-retention policy.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight (Splunkbase 7828) on the Heavy Forwarder (or the instance that runs modular inputs) and on Search Heads that need `local/props.conf` and interactive field inspection. Read Cisco’s current Splunkbase release notes before you pin, because field aliases for JSON from large MOs can shift between major TA lines.
- A Cisco Intersight API key pair (KeyId/Secret) with read access to the cond HCL and compute physical inventory paths your tenant exposes. Do not place secrets in `inputs.conf`; use the add-on’s account object so `passwords.conf` or Splunk’s secret store holds the material.
- Egress to your documented `https://intersight.com/api/v1` (or the hostname your org’s SASE policy lists) and time synchronisation: wrong NTP on the forwarder is rare for REST polling but can confuse operators comparing `_time` to the Intersight UI if you rewrite timestamps.
- Index `cisco_intersight` (name may vary) with roles granted to the compliance and platform teams, not blanket admin. Typical licence impact is small per event, but the inventory MOs and HCL documents are verbose JSON; size the index for at least 30 days if you use the posture for internal audit, longer if a regulator requests historical proof.
- A `lookups` CSV `intersight_server_inventory.csv` with `server_moid` equal to the Intersight `Moid` for each row (see Intersight URL or API), plus `hostname`, `business_service`, `owner_email`, and optional `change_freeze_until` for change-board alignment.
- Stakeholder clarity: the `Status` field is Cisco’s matrix outcome for the **host** stack, not a VMware guest compatibility statement and not a STIG or CIS check.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
Intersight model: `cond.HclStatus` is the HCL status document class; the REST list endpoint is `GET /api/v1/cond/HclStatuses`. Each result ties to a server with `ServerMoid`. The Splunk add-on flattens class names to sourcetype by lowercasing and removing the dot, producing `cisco:intersight:condhclstatus` — correct your searches if you inherited a template that incorrectly assumed `cisco:intersight:compute` and a lowercase `object_type` filter; that pattern does not match how this TA materialises the events in production.

You must also enable `compute.PhysicalSummary` (GET `/api/v1/compute/PhysicalSummaries`, sourcetype `cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary`) for names and serials, because `cond.HclStatus` alone is a technical reference without the rack and model context operators expect.

Set polling to roughly 15–30 minutes. HCL does not need second-level freshness; the risk you manage is a wrong OS+driver+driver bundle after a change, not packet loss. If you poll more often than 5 minutes, you are likely to hit account rate guardrails without improving outcomes.

Reference `inputs.conf` (path is canonical Linux; on Windows, translate under `%SPLUNK_HOME%`):

```ini
# $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight/local/inputs.conf
# Heavy Forwarder — OAuth2 account configured in the add-on, not shown here

[intersight://prod_hcl_inventory]
account = intersight_readonly_7828
endpoint = https://intersight.com/api/v1
mo_class_filter = cond.HclStatus,compute.PhysicalSummary
polling_interval = 1200
disabled = 0
sourcetype_prefix = cisco:intersight
index = cisco_intersight
```

`mo_class_filter` must include both objects or your join in Step 2 will be blind on one side. The exact key names can differ in older TA versions—mirror the shipped `default/inputs.conf` and override only the classes you need.

`props.conf` to keep JSON and timestamps predictable:

```ini
# $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/Splunk_TA_Cisco_Intersight/local/props.conf
# cond.HclStatus
[cisco:intersight:condhclstatus]
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
KV_MODE = none
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
TRUNCATE = 0
MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD = 32
TIME_FORMAT = %Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ
TZ = UTC

# compute.PhysicalSummary
[cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary]
INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json
KV_MODE = none
SHOULD_LINEMERGE = false
TRUNCATE = 0
MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD = 32
TIME_FORMAT = %Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ
TZ = UTC
```

`transforms.conf` for the business lookup, placed in a small wrapper app (for example `intersight_hcl_governance`):

```ini
# $SPLUNK_HOME/etc/apps/intersight_hcl_governance/local/transforms.conf
[intersight_server_inventory]
filename = intersight_server_inventory.csv
case_sensitive_match = false
```

`lookups/intersight_server_inventory.csv` header must be exactly `server_moid,hostname,business_service,owner_email,change_freeze_until` as used in the saved SPL. The `server_moid` string must be trimmed: leading or trailing spaces break joins silently.

Probe queries after the second poll: `index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:condhclstatus" earliest=-1d | stats count by Status` and `... sourcetype="cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary" | stats count by Model`. Zero rows on HCL with healthy inventory point to a missing `cond.HclStatus` in `mo_class_filter` or a role that cannot read the cond namespace.

### Step 2 — Create the search and alert
The SPL below is the only authoritative implementation string; the same text is stored in the `spl` field at the top of this use case. Paste it into Search, then save it as a report, a dashboard primary panel, and a scheduled alert with `cron` in the 30–60 minute range that matches your poll interval, `earliest` like `-7d@d` for a weekly posture review, or a shorter window for incident correlation.

```spl
// UC-19.1.21 — Intersight cond.HclStatus (UCS HCL: OS+driver+firmware mix; sourcetype cisco:intersight:condhclstatus; /api/v1/cond/HclStatuses; TA Splunkbase 7828)
index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:condhclstatus" earliest=-7d@d
| eval ServerMoid=coalesce(ServerMoid, server_moid, 'Server.Moid', "")
| eval hcl_state=coalesce(Status, status, "")
| eval hcl_detail=coalesce(Reason, reason, "")
| eval hcl_model=coalesce(Model, model, "")
| eval os_vendor=coalesce(OsVendor, os_vendor, "")
| eval os_vers=coalesce(OsVersion, os_version, "")
| eval srv_fw=coalesce(ServerFirmwareVersion, server_firmware_version, "")
| eval cpu_pre=coalesce(ProcessorPrefix, processor_prefix, "")
| eval hw_st=coalesce(HardwareStatus, hardware_status, "")
| eval sw_st=coalesce(SoftwareStatus, software_status, "")
| eval hcl_moid=coalesce(Moid, moid, "")
| eval tags_flat=coalesce(mvjoin(Tags, " "), tostring(Tags), "")
| eval tags_lower=lower(tags_flat)
| where hcl_state!="Validated" AND hcl_state!="" AND isnotnull(ServerMoid) AND ServerMoid!=""
| sort 0 - _time
| stats latest(_time) as last_eval_utc,
    latest(hcl_state) as hcl_status,
    latest(hcl_detail) as hcl_reason,
    latest(hcl_model) as hcl_model,
    latest(os_vendor) as os_vendor,
    latest(os_vers) as os_version,
    latest(srv_fw) as server_fw,
    latest(cpu_pre) as cpu_prefix,
    latest(hw_st) as hardware_status,
    latest(sw_st) as software_status,
    latest(tags_flat) as tags,
    latest(hcl_moid) as hcl_moid
    by ServerMoid
| eval is_prod=if(match(tags_lower, "(?i)\\bprod\\b") OR match(tags_lower, "(?i)\\bproduction\\b"), 1, 0)
| eval severity_bucket=case(
    hcl_status=="NotListed" AND is_prod==1, "critical",
    hcl_status=="NotValidated", "high",
    hcl_status=="NotListed" AND is_prod==0, "medium",
    hcl_status=="Incomplete", "low",
    hcl_status=="ServiceUnavailable", "informational",
    1=1, "medium")
| lookup intersight_server_inventory server_moid AS ServerMoid OUTPUT hostname, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until
| join type=left ServerMoid [
  search index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary" earliest=-7d@d
  | eval ServerMoid=coalesce(Moid, moid, "")
  | eval registered_name=coalesce(Name, name, "")
  | eval inv_model=coalesce(Model, model, "")
  | eval inv_serial=coalesce(Serial, serial, "")
  | where isnotnull(ServerMoid) AND ServerMoid!=""
  | stats latest(registered_name) as registered_name, latest(inv_model) as inv_model, latest(inv_serial) as inv_serial by ServerMoid
  ]
| eval display_name=coalesce(registered_name, hostname, ServerMoid)
| eval sev_n=case(severity_bucket=="critical",1, severity_bucket=="high",2, severity_bucket=="medium",3, severity_bucket=="low",4, severity_bucket=="informational",5, 1=1, 9)
| table last_eval_utc, sev_n, severity_bucket, hcl_status, display_name, ServerMoid, hcl_model, inv_model, inv_serial, os_vendor, os_version, server_fw, cpu_prefix, hardware_status, software_status, hcl_reason, hcl_moid, tags, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until
| sort 0 sev_n, hcl_status, display_name
```

How to read the pipeline: the base `search` is scoped to the TA sourcetype, not a synthetic compute sourcetype. The `coalesce` blocks are defensive for camelCase, dotted, and prior-alias spellings. `where` removes `Validated` so the alert focuses on work. The `sort` then `stats ... by ServerMoid` pattern is the per-server `dedup` the operations team actually wants. `is_prod` is a convention on `Tags` text; if your Intersight tagging uses structured JSON, consider `rex`+`sp`+`path` in a `macro` to extract an environment key and replace the `is_prod` eval—keep that macro under change control. `lookup` enriches with ownership; `join` to `cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary` is the `compute.PhysicalSummary` name and serial. `sev_n` is there so Splunk can sort a meaningful order without string collation surprises on status names.

When saving the alert, trigger on the number of results greater than zero for `severity_bucket` in ("critical","high") with throttle on `ServerMoid` and `hcl_status` of four to eight hours. Send `ServiceUnavailable` to email or a low-urgency channel unless more than a handful of `ServerMoid` values are simultaneously informational, which is your cue to check Cisco service notes.

### Step 3 — Validate
A. In the Intersight user interface, open a server you know and confirm that the HCL or health view for the host matches the `hcl_status` and `hcl_detail` in Splunk for the same Moid, allowing for poll delay.
B. In Search, `index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:condhclstatus" earliest=-1d | fieldsummary Status` to confirm the vocabulary you see in documentation (`Validated`, `NotValidated`, `NotListed`, `Incomplete`, `ServiceUnavailable`—do not hard-code a fake status the API never returns).
C. `index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:condhclstatus" earliest=-1d | eval ServerMoid=coalesce(ServerMoid, server_moid) | join type=left max=0 ServerMoid [search index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary" earliest=-1d | eval ServerMoid=coalesce(Moid, moid) | fields ServerMoid, Name, Model] | where isnull(Name)` to list any Moid the HCL stream references without a matching `PhysicalSummary`—investigate as an inventory-claim problem before you file a HCL false alarm.
D. If you have API access, call `/api/v1/cond/HclStatuses?$top=5&$select=Moid,ServerMoid,Status,Reason,ModTime` and compare the JSON to the newest Splunk event for the same `Moid` to prove parsing, not a dashboard fiction.
E. With UC-19.1.20 available, run both searches for the same `ServerMoid` in a `lookup`-style two-panel dashboard to show leadership that firmware string equality and HCL matrix validation are decoupled problems that can both fail after a change weekend.

### Step 4 — Operationalise
Name a dashboard, for example “UCS / X-Series — HCL Posture (Intersight)”. Row one: a single value for the count of `critical`+`high`. Row two: the sortable `table` from the SPL. Row three: a donut of `hcl_status` and a `clustered` or `bar` of top OS and firmware families among `NotValidated` rows. Add a data-source caption so auditors know the path is the cond HCL class, not audit logs. Wire the `owner_email` field from the lookup to your ticketing connector if your organisation’s privacy policy allows. Document that guest VM HCL and bare-metal HCL are different questions; link the Cisco public HCL page for human-readable proof outside Splunk. During change freezes, a `where` on `change_freeze_until` can suppress paging while still showing rows in the report.

Runbook: `NotValidated` — open the HCL public tool, validate whether Cisco’s matrix lists the combination as non-recommended, then plan a driver, OS minor, or firmware move that the matrix does recommend. `NotListed` in production — treat as portfolio risk: either delay workload growth, accept risk with a signed waiver, or change stack. `Incomplete` — fix telemetry: UCS Tools, guest agents, and network reachability. `ServiceUnavailable` — look for Cisco-announced HCL or cloud incidents before touching hardware. Always capture the `hcl_reason` text in the ticket; it is the fastest narrative for the platform architect.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting
If no `cisco:intersight:condhclstatus` events appear: verify `mo_class_filter` includes `cond.HclStatus`, the forwarder can reach the API, and the account role is not limited to a device group that excludes your server pool. 401/403 in `splunkd.log` for the modular input is always credential or scope, not a Splunk search-head bug.
If events appear but all fields are null: the TA version may be wrapping JSON; confirm `INDEXED_EXTRACTIONS = json` on the right sourcetype and that you did not clone an unrelated `cisco:intersight` stanza with `KV_MODE = json` that collides with indexed extractions. If the join to `cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary` is empty, compare `Moid` strings between the two sourcetypes; service-profile-only Moids may not match the HCL’s `ServerMoid` in rare deployments—Cisco TAC or your implementation guide must confirm the right relationship. If `is_prod` misfires because tag text contains both “prod” and “nonprod” labelling quirks, add an `environment` column to the CSV and `eval` from that with fallback to the regex. If `ServiceUnavailable` persists across days while other cond objects work, open a Cisco support case for the HCL back end, not a rack swap.

Long-term, export weekly CSV snapshots of the final `table` to an evidence object store, and at least annually compare your `NotListed` set against the public matrix release notes to show proactive debt reduction, not one-off heroics in incident chat.

## SPL

```spl
// UC-19.1.21 — Intersight cond.HclStatus (UCS HCL: OS+driver+firmware mix; sourcetype cisco:intersight:condhclstatus; /api/v1/cond/HclStatuses; TA Splunkbase 7828)
index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:condhclstatus" earliest=-7d@d
| eval ServerMoid=coalesce(ServerMoid, server_moid, 'Server.Moid', "")
| eval hcl_state=coalesce(Status, status, "")
| eval hcl_detail=coalesce(Reason, reason, "")
| eval hcl_model=coalesce(Model, model, "")
| eval os_vendor=coalesce(OsVendor, os_vendor, "")
| eval os_vers=coalesce(OsVersion, os_version, "")
| eval srv_fw=coalesce(ServerFirmwareVersion, server_firmware_version, "")
| eval cpu_pre=coalesce(ProcessorPrefix, processor_prefix, "")
| eval hw_st=coalesce(HardwareStatus, hardware_status, "")
| eval sw_st=coalesce(SoftwareStatus, software_status, "")
| eval hcl_moid=coalesce(Moid, moid, "")
| eval tags_flat=coalesce(mvjoin(Tags, " "), tostring(Tags), "")
| eval tags_lower=lower(tags_flat)
| where hcl_state!="Validated" AND hcl_state!="" AND isnotnull(ServerMoid) AND ServerMoid!=""
| sort 0 - _time
| stats latest(_time) as last_eval_utc,
    latest(hcl_state) as hcl_status,
    latest(hcl_detail) as hcl_reason,
    latest(hcl_model) as hcl_model,
    latest(os_vendor) as os_vendor,
    latest(os_vers) as os_version,
    latest(srv_fw) as server_fw,
    latest(cpu_pre) as cpu_prefix,
    latest(hw_st) as hardware_status,
    latest(sw_st) as software_status,
    latest(tags_flat) as tags,
    latest(hcl_moid) as hcl_moid
    by ServerMoid
| eval is_prod=if(match(tags_lower, "(?i)\\bprod\\b") OR match(tags_lower, "(?i)\\bproduction\\b"), 1, 0)
| eval severity_bucket=case(
    hcl_status=="NotListed" AND is_prod==1, "critical",
    hcl_status=="NotValidated", "high",
    hcl_status=="NotListed" AND is_prod==0, "medium",
    hcl_status=="Incomplete", "low",
    hcl_status=="ServiceUnavailable", "informational",
    1=1, "medium")
| lookup intersight_server_inventory server_moid AS ServerMoid OUTPUT hostname, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until
| join type=left ServerMoid [
  search index=cisco_intersight sourcetype="cisco:intersight:computephysicalsummary" earliest=-7d@d
  | eval ServerMoid=coalesce(Moid, moid, "")
  | eval registered_name=coalesce(Name, name, "")
  | eval inv_model=coalesce(Model, model, "")
  | eval inv_serial=coalesce(Serial, serial, "")
  | where isnotnull(ServerMoid) AND ServerMoid!=""
  | stats latest(registered_name) as registered_name, latest(inv_model) as inv_model, latest(inv_serial) as inv_serial by ServerMoid
  ]
| eval display_name=coalesce(registered_name, hostname, ServerMoid)
| eval sev_n=case(severity_bucket=="critical",1, severity_bucket=="high",2, severity_bucket=="medium",3, severity_bucket=="low",4, severity_bucket=="informational",5, 1=1, 9)
| table last_eval_utc, sev_n, severity_bucket, hcl_status, display_name, ServerMoid, hcl_model, inv_model, inv_serial, os_vendor, os_version, server_fw, cpu_prefix, hardware_status, software_status, hcl_reason, hcl_moid, tags, business_service, owner_email, change_freeze_until
| sort 0 sev_n, hcl_status, display_name
```

## Visualization

Per-server HCL status table (colour by `severity_bucket`) with `display_name`, `hcl_status`, `hcl_reason`, and OS and firmware columns; a donut of `hcl_status` for non-`Validated` only; a cluster bar of top `(os_vendor, os_vers, server_fw)` triples for `NotValidated` to guide bundle and driver work; a companion single-value of critical plus high count in the last poll window; optional timechart of `ServiceUnavailable` share to catch tenant-side HCL service issues.

## Known False Positives

A lab or pre-production cluster that deliberately runs a driver or OS ahead of the published matrix will show `NotListed` or `NotValidated` even when your team is qualifying it under change control, so route those serials with a `nonprod` or `hcl_waiver` column in the lookup rather than sev-1 pages. The first day after a bare-metal or profile rebuild often shows `Incomplete` while UCS Tools and guest telemetry back-fill—suppress short windows, not the signal forever. A transient `ServiceUnavailable` during Intersight or HCL back-end maintenance can flip many servers in one poll; treat multi-host correlation as infrastructure noise unless it persists. OEM network or FC HBAs and third-party NVMe-oF adapters may sit outside Cisco’s HCL for the exact firmware you chose even when the team considers them “certified in practice.” Planned PSIRT remediations that temporarily park firmware on a non-recommended build may trip `NotValidated` until the matrix catches up—pair with a ticket id in the lookup. Cross-vendor qualification clusters for ISVs will intentionally violate HCL; track them in a `business_service` that routes to an informational only queue.

- [Cisco DevNet — cond.HclStatus (Intersight model relationships)](https://developer.cisco.com/docs/intersight/cond-hclstatus-relationship/)
- [Cisco UCS Hardware and Software Compatibility (HCL tool, public)](https://ucshcltool.cloudapps.cisco.com/public/)
- [Cisco UCS Interoperability and compatibility (product hub)](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/products/servers-unified-computing/interoperability.html)
- [Splunk Add-on for Cisco Intersight (Splunkbase 7828)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7828)
